items.title: Third Australian bird flu case confirmed, suspected case in Dunsborough

children.content: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5N1 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast, as another suspected case emerges in WA.

articles.title: PNG reverses bird flu ban on Australian chicken and egg imports

children.content: Australia's largest chicken meat buyer, Papua New Guinea, has reversed its decision to suspend
Australian poultry and egg imports due to the H5N1 bird flu outbreak.

image.altText: Dozens of hens with glossy brown feathers and red plump combs under a clear blue sky.

articles.title: Australia's mammals are at risk of bird flu. This is what we know

children.content: While birds are the most likely victims of the deadly strain of bird flu, researchers are also sounding the alarm for native mammals and marsupial species. These are some of the steps being taken to protect them.

image.altText: small black animal with white stripe across chest looking at straight at the viewer, standing over dead animal

items.title: Tech billionaire accused of assaulting partner, cutting $66k Birkin bag with scissors

image.altText: A man in a suit walking down a street as two reporters point microphones towards him

children.content: A billionaire tech investor accused of assaulting his former partner and stealing expensive gifts he had bought her has his bail varied so he can visit his mother in Sydney.

items.title: Gina Rinehart names veteran accommodation after Ben Roberts-Smith

children.content: Australia's richest person has opened a facility for homeless veterans in a beachside Perth suburb.

image.altText: An apartment building with "Ben Roberts-Smith Beach Houses" written on the side in large writing

items.title: WA Police to review case of Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre

children.content: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

image.altText: woman with white blonde hair and wearing a navy suit looks down the camera

items.title: Man, 76, dies in plane crash near Northam, north-east of Perth

image.altText: Aerials of a light plane crash.

children.content: Police say a light plane crashed in Malabaine on Wednesday morning, with the pilot and sole occupant of the aircraft found dead at the scene.

items.title: How Perth girl got caught in crossfire of 'deliberate policy of lethal force'

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

gallery.altText: Headshot of a father and his young daughter holding up a graduation certificate.
Selfie of a man hugging his young daughter and forming finger signs.
A grieving older man covers his eyes while seated on a grey sofa.

items.title: Concerns raised about WA worker shortages amid vocational training drop

children.content: The number of West Australians enrolled in apprenticeships and traineeships is the lowest in five years after a post-COVID spike in numbers.

image.altText: Man with helmet climbs on scaffolding

items.title: Asteroid crater in Pilbara confirmed again as world's oldest

children.content: Researchers find a crater in WA's East Pilbara is 3 billion years old, but it has sparked debate in the scientific community.

image.altText: Rocky hills surrounding a flattish plain. The rocks and dirt are red. Small shrubs are scattered around.

items.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson taken offline

children.content: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

image.altText: Two men in black on a street.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says vision of 'monoculture' is like the Socceroos

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has likened her vision of a "monocultural" Australia to the Socceroos as Opposition Leader Angus Taylor clarifies that he would still seek "a version" of multiculturalism.

image.altText: Socceroos players sing the national anthem

items.title: Socceroos should make 'harsh' call for final group game, Schwarzer says

children.content: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play Paraguay and what time is the game?

children.content: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Irankunda slides along a green pitch on his knees.

articles.title: 'Doesn't seem right': Why Socceroos won't play for a draw against Paraguay

children.content: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

image.altText: Jordan Bos #5 of Australia is tackled by Hakan Calhanoglu #10 of Turkiye

articles.title: Quick hits: Trump's special job and commentator banned for outburst

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo makes a bit of World Cup history as Gianni Infantino offers an update on the future of the hydration breaks and Donald Trump is given role at this year's final. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Donald Trump, Cristiano Ronaldo, Gianni Infantino

thumbnail.altText: Socceroo Mark Schwarzer shouts encouragement to his team-mates during the 2010 FIFA World Cup qualifying match

items.title: Qantas A380 to be inspected urgently after cracks found

children.content: Airbus says it will inspect 16 A380 planes, including one Qantas plane, after cracks were found in a key wing component.

image.altText: A Virgin and a Qantas airliner on a tarmac.

items.title: Christian Brothers spending $1.7m a week on abuse cases

children.content: The Congregation of Christian Brothers Oceania Province has revealed it was seeking to sell off its 36 properties to pay abuse victims, who would be treated as creditors.

image.altText: A wooden cross at the top of a building with blue sky behind it.

items.title: Heartbroken Daryl Braithwaite will miss everything about performing

children.content: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

image.altText: Daryl Braithwaite

items.title: Bondi terror attack hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on father

children.content: Ahmed Al Ahmed has pleaded not guilty to allegations he assaulted and intimidated his father during an incident in March.

image.altText: Man in dark suit, white shirt looking down, another man with beard standing next to him

items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

image.altText: A van carries people in the dark

gallery.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

thumbnail.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

items.title: Australians financially supporting family in Lebanon say they have no other option

children.content: Lebanese Australians, who have spent thousands of dollars to help their loved ones survive a two-and-a-half-year war, bear a financial burden that also comes with an emotional toll.

image.altText: A photo of Sue (Far right) with her family pictured behind them in Lebanon.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

image.altText: A composite image of a woman smiling with a needle and a woman taking a selfie.

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, Noongar Boodjar actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".
